subject_options: Arnos Bistrot and your 293 Google reviews
Getting more from Arnos Bistrot's search presence
One gap worth knowing about for Arnos Bistrot
email_body:
I came across Arnos Bistrot while researching hospitality businesses in Westport, and your Google reputation stood out — 4.9 stars across 293 reviews is genuinely rare in this market.

One thing I noticed is that those reviews are displayed manually on the site rather than pulled through dynamically. At the same time, your mobile PageSpeed sits at 65, which is the point where visiting tourists are most likely searching. Both are relatively contained fixes that tend to have a direct effect on how often the site converts a browser into a booking.

I work with a small agency that focuses on exactly this kind of targeted local SEO work — schema markup, Google Reviews embedding, and mobile performance — scoped as a fixed project rather than an ongoing retainer.

I would be happy to send over a one-page summary of the specific changes and what they could mean for bookings, or we could walk through it on a short call whenever suits you.

Best regards
[Your name]
cta: I would be happy to send over a one-page summary of the specific changes and what they could mean for bookings, or we could walk through it on a short call whenever suits you.
follow_up_body: I wanted to follow up briefly on my note from last week about Arnos Bistrot's search presence. The core idea was a fixed-scope project covering Google Reviews embedding, schema markup, and a mobile performance improvement — nothing open-ended. I am conscious it may not be the right time, but I wanted to check whether this is something worth exploring, or if there is a better person on your side to connect with regarding the website. Happy to work around whatever suits.
